title: When Youï؟½re Bad In Your Inside
content:
آ آ آ آ I remarked that man is saddest, and his heart is filled with woe,
آ آ آ آ When he hasnï؟½t any money, and his pants begin to go;
آ آ آ آ But I think I was mistaken, and there are many times I find
آ آ آ آ When you do not care a candle if your pants are gone behind;
آ آ آ آ For a fellow mostly loses all ambition, hope, and pride,
آ آ آ آ When, to put the matter mildly, he is bad in his inside.

آ آ آ آ Bobby Burns was down on toothache, and it troubled him no doubt;
آ آ آ آ But you know a man can always have a molar taken out,
آ آ آ آ And be all right then, excepting for the duller pain that comes
آ آ آ آ To the hollow that is lying like a gully in the gums.
آ آ آ آ But you canï؟½t extract your innards, they must stay within your hide,
آ آ آ آ And youï؟½ve got to moan and cuss it, when youï؟½re bad in your inside.

آ آ آ آ You dunno what to take for it, you dunno what to do:
آ آ آ آ You are puzzled to remember what has disagreed with you,
آ آ آ آ You lie in all positions, there is none will give you ease;
آ آ آ آ And you think an aching stomach is the king of agonies.
آ آ آ آ You feel as though your innards in a double knot are tied,
آ آ آ آ While the devil ties it tighter, when youï؟½re bad in your inside.

آ آ آ آ Then you send that boy, that Harry, and you tell him to be quick,
آ آ آ آ For a shillingï؟½s worth of brandy, ï؟½for a person who is sickï؟½.
آ آ آ آ You make him swear to hurry, and he goes off like a shot;
آ آ آ آ But you wait an hour and suffer, and the brandy cometh not;
آ آ آ آ Then you look out through the window, and you swear to bust his hide,
آ آ آ آ For the wretch is playing football, while youï؟½re bad in your inside.

آ آ آ آ Then thereï؟½s mostly some old woman, with your aunt or mother, too,
آ آ آ آ And itï؟½s really quite indecent how she cross-examines you.
آ آ آ آ She insists on giving physic, and will hear of no excuse;
آ آ آ آ And dilates upon your bowels till you wish her to the deuce.
آ آ آ آ You wish sheï؟½d go and leave you, let you be and let it slide,
آ آ آ آ And go about her business, when youï؟½re bad in your inside.

آ آ آ آ But sheï؟½s come to see you through it, and she bustles in and out;
آ آ آ آ And she talks of private matters that she oughtnï؟½t talk about.
آ آ آ آ She proceeds to pill and dose you, and she vows that youï؟½ll be ill
آ آ آ آ Till youï؟½ve swallowed every nostrum, castor oil, and draught and pill,
آ آ آ آ And you wish, good Lord! that she would pass across the Stygian tide,
آ آ آ آ And nurse the gory Devil, when heï؟½s bad in his inside.

آ آ آ آ But the hag is interested, and she bustles out and in;
آ آ آ آ And in various disguises give you nauseous medicine.
آ آ آ آ Till sheï؟½s shifted all obstructions, and has soothed your keenest pain
آ آ آ آ (Though her remedies may leave you a much sicker man again);
آ آ آ آ But sheï؟½s done her best to help you, for her sympathy is wide,
آ آ آ آ And youï؟½ll bless that same old woman when youï؟½re right in your inside.
meaning: About: Henry Lawson (17 June 1867 - 2 September 1922) was an Australian writer and poet. Along with his contemporary Banjo Paterson, Lawson is among the best-known Australian poets and fiction writers of the colonial period.
